tasks.title: Reset Project
tasks.description:
OCHO PROJECT BRIEF: Life Decision Dashboard Using Prediction Markets

Goal:
Build a Node.js dashboard that pulls open/public market data from Polymarket and Kalshi, filters out sports, groups markets into life-decision spheres, and converts market prices into decision intelligence that can inform real-world decisions across business, politics, climate, technology, economy, AI, and related domains.

Primary categories:
- Business
- Politics
- Climate
- Technology
- Economy
- AI
- Geopolitics
- Health/public policy
- Energy
- Regulation
- Housing/real estate if available

Explicit exclusions:
- Sports
- Pure entertainment unless it materially impacts business or culture
- Meme or novelty markets
- Low-liquidity or low-signal markets

PRIMARY API SOURCES

Polymarket:
Base endpoint:
https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/markets

Use query params:
- active=true
- closed=false
- limit=500
- order=volume24hr
- ascending=false

Also evaluate:
- events endpoints
- condition IDs
- outcome prices (YES/NO)
- volume
- liquidity
- tags/categories
- end dates

Kalshi:
Use public endpoints documented at:
https://docs.kalshi.com

Core concepts:
- Events
- Markets
- Series
- Order books
- YES/NO pricing
- Volume
- Open interest
- Settlement rules

AGENT ROLES

Ocho (Orchestrator):
Owns architecture, sequencing, integration, scope control, QA, and final delivery. Ocho is the only agent that communicates externally.

Cinco (Web Fetcher):
Discovers and validates Polymarket and Kalshi APIs, pulls sample payloads, identifies field structures, documents quirks, rate limits, and categorization signals.

Siete (Data Analyst):
Designs taxonomy, normalization schema, scoring models, confidence metrics, and decision translation logic. Owns data integrity and signal quality.

Diez (Coder):
Builds the Node.js application, API clients, backend routes, normalization pipeline, database, caching layer, and frontend dashboard.

PRODUCT CONCEPT

Core question:
“What is the world currently pricing in, and how should that influence decisions?”

Core views:
1. World Probability Dashboard
2. Category Dashboards (Business, Politics, Climate, Technology, etc.)
3. Life Decision Dashboard
4. Market Detail View
5. Trend/Momentum View
6. Alerts + Change Detection

LIFE DECISION USE CASES

Career:
- AI adoption trajectory
- hiring vs layoffs expectations
- industry strength signals

Finance:
- recession probability
- rate cuts/hikes expectations
- inflation persistence

Relocation:
- climate risk signals
- energy pricing expectations
- regulatory/political stability

Business:
- regulatory changes
- industry disruption
- macroeconomic direction

Climate:
- extreme weather signals
- emissions/policy signals
- long-term risk indicators

Technology:
- AI milestones
- semiconductor constraints
- regulatory environment

DATA PIPELINE

1. Fetch Layer (Diez builds, Cinco informs)
- Pull active markets from Polymarket
- Pull active markets from Kalshi
- Store raw payloads for inspection

2. Filter Layer
- Remove sports markets via tags/title filtering
- Remove closed/resolved markets
- Remove low-liquidity markets
- Remove ambiguous or low-quality markets

3. Classification Layer (Siete defines)
Map markets into:
- business
- politics
- climate
- technology
- economy
- AI
- geopolitics
- health
- regulation
- energy
- housing

4. Probability Layer
- Convert YES price to implied probability
- Use midpoint of bid/ask when available
- Track spread
- Track liquidity and volume
- Track time-based movement

5. Decision Layer
Translate probabilities into structured decision signals

NORMALIZED MARKET OBJECT

{
  source: "polymarket" | "kalshi",
  sourceMarketId: string,
  eventId: string,
  title: string,
  description: string,
  category: string,
  tags: string[],
  sphere: string[],
  yesPrice: number,
  noPrice: number,
  impliedProbability: number,
  bid: number,
  ask: number,
  spread: number,
  volume: number,
  liquidity: number,
  openInterest: number,
  closeDate: string,
  resolutionDate: string,
  url: string,
  status: "active" | "closed" | "resolved",
  lastUpdated: string
}

SIGNAL OBJECT

{
  marketId: string,
  sphere: string,
  probability: number,
  confidence: number,
  importance: 1-5,
  liquidityScore: number,
  movement24h: number,
  movement7d: number,
  decisionImpact: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical",
  summary: string,
  suggestedConsideration: string
}

SCORING MODEL (Siete)

SignalStrength =
  impliedProbability
  × liquidityWeight
  × volumeWeight
  × recencyWeight
  × importanceWeight

Confidence increases when:
- high liquidity
- narrow spread
- high volume
- cross-market agreement
- cross-platform agreement (Kalshi vs Polymarket)

Confidence decreases when:
- low liquidity
- wide spread
- ambiguous resolution
- weak category relevance

DASHBOARD STRUCTURE

1. Macro Worldview
- recession odds
- inflation/rate expectations
- major political outcomes
- AI/regulation signals
- climate risks

2. Category Dashboards
Each category shows:
- top markets
- probability distributions
- recent movement
- key insights

3. Personal Decision Dashboard
Cards:
- Career
- Finance
- Business
- Relocation
- Climate
- Technology

Each card includes:
- top signals
- changes in last 24h/7d
- interpretation
- decision implication
- confidence score

4. Market Table
Columns:
- Market
- Source
- Sphere
- Probability
- Movement
- Volume
- Liquidity
- Close date
- Confidence
- Impact

LOCAL API ROUTES (Diez)

GET /api/markets
GET /api/markets?sphere=technology
GET /api/signals
GET /api/spheres
GET /api/life-decisions
GET /api/market/:id
GET /api/trends
GET /api/alerts

FRONTEND

Stack:
- React + Vite or Next.js
- Tailwind
- TypeScript

Components:
- GlobalDashboard
- CategoryView
- MarketTable
- SignalFeed
- DecisionCards
- MarketDetailModal
- TrendCharts

Include:
- auto-refresh
- last updated timestamp
- loading and error states
- responsive layout

DATA STORAGE

Use:
- SQLite or Postgres
- in-memory cache for live data

Tables:
- markets
- market_snapshots
- signals
- alerts

Snapshot every 15 minutes:
- probability
- spread
- volume
- liquidity

ALERT RULES

Trigger when:
- probability changes ≥ 10% in 24h
- crosses 50% or 70% thresholds
- liquidity spikes
- cross-platform disagreement ≥ 15%
- new high-volume market appears

EXECUTION ORDER

1. Ocho: define MVP and architecture
2. Cinco: research APIs and capture sample payloads
3. Siete: define schemas and scoring model
4. Diez: scaffold Node + frontend project
5. Diez: build Polymarket client
6. Diez: build Kalshi client
7. Diez: build normalization pipeline
8. Diez: build database and snapshot system
9. Diez: implement API routes
10. Diez: build frontend UI
11. Siete: validate scoring and classification
12. Cinco: validate API accuracy and edge cases
13. Ocho: final QA and integration
14. Ocho: produce README and deployment guide

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- App runs via npm install and npm run dev
- Successfully pulls Polymarket data
- Successfully pulls Kalshi data
- Filters out sports
- Categorizes into defined spheres
- Displays probabilities and trends
- Stores historical snapshots
- Generates decision cards
- Handles API failures gracefully
- Includes full documentation

GUARDRAILS

- Do not provide betting advice
- Do not treat probabilities as certainty
- Always display liquidity, spread, and source
- Always include confidence scoring
- Clearly surface uncertainty and limitations

FINAL DELIVERABLE

A fully functional Node.js application that:
- aggregates non-sports prediction markets
- normalizes and scores them
- surfaces meaningful signals
- translates them into decision intelligence
- tracks changes over time
- presents actionable insights in a clean dashboard

Ocho must deliver:
- working app
- architecture overview
- API documentation
- known limitations
- recommended next iterations

tasks.title: API research + sample payloads (Polymarket + Kalshi)
tasks.description: Fetch current docs/endpoints for Polymarket gamma API and Kalshi public API; capture sample JSON; note key fields, pagination, rate limits, and tags/categories useful to filter sports.

tasks.title: Taxonomy + scoring model draft
tasks.description:
Define sphere taxonomy mapping rules, confidence scoring, and how to translate market probabilities into decision signals; output design notes and examples.

[Note] Siete wrote only a stub checklist at design/taxonomy-scoring.md; needs full content per spec.

tasks.title: Project scaffold sanity check + run instructions update
tasks.description: Open repo, verify dev server/build scripts; propose minimal scaffolding changes needed to support data pipeline and dashboard views; provide patch if needed.
